entries.name: dateCellRangeOfTimingFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellRangeOfTimingFactory} that converts dates or indexes into a clamped
{@link DateCellRangeWithRange} relative to the configured timing.

When `fitToTimingRange` is true (default), the returned range is clamped to the timing's valid index bounds.
When `limitToCompletedIndexes` is true, only indexes whose timing duration has fully elapsed are included,
with the max boundary lazily refreshed as time passes.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration specifying the timing, range fit, and completion constraints.

entries.returns: A factory function that produces a clamped {@link DateCellRangeWithRange } from optional start/end input.

entries.name: dateCellRangeOfTiming
entries.signature: dateCellRangeOfTiming(config: DateCellTiming | DateCallIndexRangeFromDatesFactoryConfig, input?: Maybe<DateCellRangeOfTimingInput>): DateCellRangeWithRange
entries.description:
Computes a {@link DateCellRangeWithRange} from a timing and optional start/end input.

Shorthand for creating a {@link dateCellRangeOfTimingFactory} and immediately invoking it.

params.name: config
params.description: - A {@link DateCellTiming } or full factory configuration.

params.name: input
params.description: - Optional start/end boundaries for the range.

entries.returns: A clamped {@link DateCellRangeWithRange } derived from the timing and input.

entries.name: dateCellTimingCompletedTimeRange
entries.description:
Returns a {@link DateCellRangeWithRange} representing only the completed (fully elapsed) portion
of the timing schedule. Useful for determining which days have already finished.

By default fitToTimingRange is true.

params.name: timing
params.description: - The timing schedule to evaluate.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for the current time reference and range fitting.

entries.returns: A {@link DateCellRangeWithRange } covering only the completed day indexes.

entries.name: dateCellTimingLatestCompletedIndex
entries.description:
Returns the latest completed day index for a {@link DateCellTiming}.

Returns -1 if no days have been completed yet.

params.name: timing
params.description: - The timing schedule to evaluate.

params.name: now
params.description: - Optional reference time; defaults to the current time.

entries.returns: The zero-based index of the last fully completed day, or -1 if none.

entries.name: dateCellRangeToDateCellIndexRange
entries.description: Converts a {@link DateCellRange} (inclusive `to`) to a {@link DateCellIndexRange} (exclusive `maxIndex`).

params.name: range
params.description: - The inclusive date cell range to convert.

entries.returns: A {@link DateCellIndexRange } with exclusive `maxIndex`.

entries.name: dateCellIndexRangeToDateCellRange
entries.description: Converts a {@link DateCellIndexRange} (exclusive `maxIndex`) back to a {@link DateCellRangeWithRange} (inclusive `to`).

params.name: range
params.description: - The exclusive date cell index range to convert.

entries.returns: A {@link DateCellRangeWithRange } with inclusive `to`.

entries.name: dateCellIndexRange
entries.signature: dateCellIndexRange(timing: DateCellTiming, limit?: DateCellTimingRangeInput, fitToTimingRange?: boolean): DateCellIndexRange
entries.description:
Generates a {@link DateCellIndexRange} based on the input timing.

An arbitrary limit can also be applied. When `fitToTimingRange` is true (default),
the limit is intersected with the timing's own range; otherwise the limit is used as-is.

params.name: timing
params.description: - The timing schedule to derive the range from.

params.name: limit
params.description: - Optional range input to constrain the output.

params.name: fitToTimingRange
params.description: - Whether to intersect the limit with the timing's own range. Defaults to true.

entries.returns: A {@link DateCellIndexRange } representing the computed index bounds.

entries.name: expandDateCellCollection
entries.description:
Expands a {@link DateCellCollection} into an array of {@link DateCellDurationSpan} values
by combining its timing and blocks.

Shorthand for calling {@link expandDateCellTiming} with `collection.timing` and `collection.blocks`.

params.name: collection
params.description: - Collection whose timing and blocks should be expanded.

entries.returns: Duration spans with concrete start times for each block.

entries.name: expandDateCellTiming
entries.description:
Expands the given blocks into {@link DateCellDurationSpan} values using the provided timing.

Shorthand for creating a {@link dateCellTimingExpansionFactory} and immediately invoking it.

params.name: timing
params.description: - Schedule providing start times and duration.

params.name: blocks
params.description: - Blocks to expand into concrete duration spans.

entries.returns: Duration spans with concrete start times.

entries.name: dateCellTimingExpansionFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingExpansionFactory} that expands date cell blocks into
{@link DateCellDurationSpan} values with concrete start times and durations.

Blocks with a range (`i` to `to`) are expanded into individual single-index entries.
Filtering is applied both at the block level and at the computed duration span level,
and evaluation can be capped for performance with large datasets.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration specifying the timing, range limits, filters, and output caps.

entries.returns: A factory function that expands date cell blocks into {@link DateCellDurationSpan } arrays.

entries.name: dateCellDayTimingInfoFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellDayTimingInfoFactory} that computes detailed timing information
(progress state, start/end times, range membership) for any given date or day index.

The factory handles timezone normalization internally and accounts for edge cases
where a timing window spans midnight into the next day.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration providing the timing and optional range limit.

entries.returns: A factory that computes {@link DateCellDayTimingInfo } for any date or day index.

entries.name: isDateCellTimingRelativeIndexFactory
entries.description:
Type guard that returns true if the input is a {@link DateCellTimingRelativeIndexFactory}.

Checks for the presence of `_timing` and `_normalInstance` properties on a function.

params.name: input
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: True if the input is a {@link DateCellTimingRelativeIndexFactory }.

entries.name: dateCellTimingRelativeIndexFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingRelativeIndexFactory} that converts dates, ISO8601 day strings,
or indexes into a zero-based day index relative to the timing's start date.

If an existing factory is passed, it is returned as-is (idempotent). The factory normalizes
all date inputs through UTC to handle timezone offsets correctly, computing the floor
of the hour difference divided by 24 to determine the day offset.

params.name: input
params.description: - A timing configuration or an existing factory (returned as-is).

entries.returns: A factory that converts dates, ISO8601 day strings, or indexes to zero-based day offsets.

entries.name: dateCellTimingRelativeIndexArrayFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingRelativeIndexArrayFactory} that converts mixed arrays of
dates, date ranges, and cell ranges into a flat array of day indexes.

Date ranges and cell ranges are expanded to include every index within the range.

params.name: indexFactory
params.description: - The relative index factory used for date-to-index conversion.

entries.returns: A factory that flattens mixed date/range arrays into day index arrays.

entries.name: getRelativeIndexForDateCellTiming
entries.description:
Convenience function that returns the zero-based day index for a date (or index)
relative to the given timing's start.

Defaults to the current date/time if no date is provided.

params.name: timing
params.description: - The timing providing the start date and timezone context.

params.name: date
params.description: - Moment to convert; defaults to the current date/time.

entries.returns: The zero-based day index relative to the timing's start.

entries.name: dateCellTimingDateFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingDateFactory} that maps day indexes to calendar dates
while preserving the current time-of-day.

This is useful when you need the actual Date for a given index (e.g., for display or
date arithmetic) but want to retain the hours/minutes of "now" rather than using
the timing's startsAt time.

params.name: timing
params.description: - The timing providing the start date and timezone context.

entries.returns: A factory that maps day indexes to calendar dates preserving the current time-of-day.

entries.name: dateCellTimingEndIndex
entries.description:
Returns the last (maximum) day index for a {@link DateCellTiming} schedule.

This is the index corresponding to the timing's `end` date, representing the final
day in the schedule.

params.name: input
params.description: - A timing or an existing relative index factory.

entries.returns: The zero-based index of the last day in the schedule.
entries.example:
const timing = dateCellTiming({ startsAt, duration: 60 }, 5);
const lastIndex = dateCellTimingEndIndex(timing); // 4 (zero-based, 5 days)

entries.name: dateCellTimingStartDateFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingStartDateFactory} that computes the calendar start-of-day date
for any day index relative to the timing's start.

The returned date represents the beginning of the day in the timing's timezone context.

params.name: input
params.description: - A timing or an existing relative index factory.

entries.returns: A factory that maps day indexes to the start-of-day date in the timing's timezone.

entries.name: dateCellTimingStartsAtDateFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingStartsAtDateFactory} that computes the concrete event start time
for any day index relative to the timing's schedule.

The returned date reflects the actual `startsAt` time-of-day offset to the correct calendar date
for the requested index, with proper timezone normalization.

params.name: input
params.description: - A timing or an existing relative index factory.

entries.returns: A factory that computes the concrete event start time for any day index.

entries.name: dateCellTimingEndDateFactory
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DateCellTimingEndDateFactory} that computes the end time
(startsAt + duration) for any day index relative to the timing's schedule.

params.name: input
params.description: - A timing or an existing relative index factory.

entries.returns: A factory that computes the end time (startsAt + duration) for any day index.

entries.name: getRelativeDateForDateCellTiming
entries.description:
Convenience function that returns the calendar date for a given day index or date
relative to the timing. Shorthand for creating a {@link dateCellTimingDateFactory} and invoking it.

params.name: timing
params.description: - The timing providing the start date and timezone context.

params.name: input
params.description: - Moment or day-index to resolve to a calendar date.

entries.returns: The calendar date corresponding to the input, preserving current time-of-day.

entries.name: updateDateCellTimingWithDateCellTimingEvent
entries.description:
Produces a new {@link FullDateCellTiming} by selectively replacing parts of an existing timing
with values from a {@link DateCellTimingEvent}.

This is the primary mechanism for updating a timing schedule in response to user edits.
The `replaceStartDay`, `replaceStartsAt`, `endOnEvent`, and `replaceDuration` flags
independently control which aspects of the timing are modified, and can be combined.

When both `replaceStartDay` and `replaceStartsAt` are true, the event's startsAt is used directly.
When only `replaceStartDay` is true, the day changes but the time-of-day is preserved.
When only `replaceStartsAt` is true, the time-of-day changes but the calendar date is preserved.

params.name: input
params.description: - Configuration specifying the timing to update, the event source, and which aspects to replace.

entries.returns: A new {@link FullDateCellTiming } with the requested aspects replaced.

entries.name: isDateWithinDateCellRangeFunction
entries.description:
Creates a predicate that checks whether a date, index, or range falls within
the configured reference range.

Converts all date-based inputs to cell indexes using the configured (or inferred) timezone
before performing the containment check.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration specifying the reference range and optional timezone context.

entries.returns: A predicate function that checks containment within the configured range.

entries.name: DATE_CELL_SCHEDULE_ENCODED_WEEK_REGEX
entries.description:
Regex that loosely validates a {@link DateCellScheduleEncodedWeek}.

Matches zero-to-nine digit characters (any of `0-9`). Does not enforce ascending order or uniqueness of
digits — use {@link isDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek} for a runtime check that mirrors the type, or
{@link dateCellScheduleEncodedWeek} to build a guaranteed-valid encoding from {@link DateCellScheduleDayCode}
values.
entries.returns: RegExp

entries.name: isDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek
entries.description:
Type guard that returns true if the input matches the {@link DATE_CELL_SCHEDULE_ENCODED_WEEK_REGEX} encoded-week format.

The regex check is loose — it allows duplicate digits and any digit ordering. To produce a canonically simplified
encoding, use {@link dateCellScheduleEncodedWeek}.

params.name: input
params.description: - String to validate against the encoded week regex.

entries.returns: Whether the string matches the encoded week format.
entries.example:
isDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('24');  // true (Mon + Wed)
isDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('8');   // true (WEEKDAY shorthand)
isDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('');    // true (no days)
isDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('abc'); // false

entries.name: isEmptyDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek
entries.description:
Returns true if the input represents an empty (no days selected) {@link DateCellScheduleEncodedWeek}.

Both the empty string `''` and `'0'` are treated as empty since `0` corresponds to
{@link DateCellScheduleDayCode.NONE}, the no-op day code.

params.name: input
params.description: - String to check for emptiness.

entries.returns: Whether the encoded week represents no selected days.
entries.example:
isEmptyDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('');  // true
isEmptyDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('0'); // true (NONE code)
isEmptyDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('8'); // false (WEEKDAY)
isEmptyDateCellScheduleEncodedWeek('24'); // false (Mon + Wed)

entries.name: dateCellScheduleEncodedWeek
entries.description:
Creates a simplified {@link DateCellScheduleEncodedWeek} from an iterable of {@link DateCellScheduleDayCode} values.

Codes are passed through {@link simplifyDateCellScheduleDayCodes} so redundant individual day codes are collapsed
into shorthand (e.g. Mon-Fri becomes WEEKDAY `8`, Sat+Sun becomes WEEKEND `9`) and digits are emitted in ascending order.

params.name: codes
params.description: - Day codes to encode into the compact string representation.

entries.returns: The encoded week string.

entries.name: timeDurationDataIsEmpty
entries.description: Returns true if the input TimeDurationData has no meaningful values (all zero or undefined).

params.name: data
params.description: - The duration data to check.

entries.returns: True if empty.
entries.example:
timeDurationDataIsEmpty({}); // true
timeDurationDataIsEmpty({ hours: 0, minutes: 0 }); // true
timeDurationDataIsEmpty({ hours: 1 }); // false

entries.name: durationDataToMilliseconds
entries.description: Converts a TimeDurationData to total milliseconds by summing all fields.

params.name: data
params.description: - The duration data to convert.

entries.returns: Total milliseconds.
entries.example:
durationDataToMilliseconds({ hours: 1, minutes: 30 }); // 5400000
durationDataToMilliseconds({ days: 1, hours: 2 }); // 93600000

entries.name: millisecondsToDurationData
entries.signature: millisecondsToDurationData(ms: Milliseconds, units?: readonly TimeUnit[]): TimeDurationData
entries.description:
Decomposes milliseconds into a TimeDurationData object using the specified units.

Breaks down from largest to smallest unit, only using units in the provided list.

params.name: ms
params.description: - The total milliseconds to decompose.

params.name: units
params.description: - Which units to decompose into (defaults to days, hours, minutes, seconds)

entries.returns: A TimeDurationData with the decomposed values.
entries.example:
millisecondsToDurationData(5400000); // { days: 0, hours: 1, minutes: 30, seconds: 0 }
millisecondsToDurationData(90000, ['min', 's']); // { minutes: 1, seconds: 30 }

entries.name: getDurationDataValue
entries.description: Reads a specific time unit value from a TimeDurationData object.

params.name: data
params.description: - The duration data.

params.name: unit
params.description: - The time unit to read.

entries.returns: The value for that unit, or 0 if not set.

entries.name: setDurationDataValue
entries.signature: setDurationDataValue(data: TimeDurationData, unit: TimeUnit, value: number): TimeDurationData
entries.description: Returns a new TimeDurationData with the specified unit set to the given value.

params.name: data
params.description: - The original duration data.

params.name: unit
params.description: - The time unit to set.

params.name: value
params.description: - The new value.

entries.returns: A new TimeDurationData with the updated value.

entries.name: parseDurationString
entries.description:
Parses a human-readable duration string into a TimeDurationData object.

Supports compact formats ("3d10h5m8s"), spaced formats ("3d 10h 5m 8s"),
and long formats ("3 days 10 hours 5 minutes 8 seconds"). Mixed formats
are also supported. If the same unit appears multiple times, values are summed.

If the string contains only a number with no unit, it is treated as the
smallest unit that would make sense (milliseconds by default).

params.name: input
params.description: - The duration string to parse.

entries.returns: A TimeDurationData object with the parsed values.

entries.name: parseDurationStringToMilliseconds
entries.description: Parses a duration string directly to milliseconds.

params.name: input
params.description: - The duration string to parse.

entries.returns: Total milliseconds.
entries.example: parseDurationStringToMilliseconds('1h30m'); // 5400000

entries.name: formatDurationString
entries.description:
Formats a TimeDurationData to a compact string like "3d10h5m8s".

Omits zero-value units. Returns "0s" if all fields are zero or empty.

params.name: data
params.description: - The duration data to format.

entries.returns: A compact duration string.
entries.example:
formatDurationString({ days: 3, hours: 10, minutes: 5, seconds: 8 }); // "3d10h5m8s"
formatDurationString({ hours: 2, minutes: 30 }); // "2h30m"
formatDurationString({}); // "0s"

entries.name: formatDurationStringLong
entries.description:
Formats a TimeDurationData to a long human-readable string.

Omits zero-value units. Returns "0 seconds" if all fields are zero or empty.

params.name: data
params.description: - The duration data to format.

entries.returns: A human-readable duration string.
entries.example:
formatDurationStringLong({ days: 3, hours: 10 }); // "3 days 10 hours"
formatDurationStringLong({ hours: 1, minutes: 1 }); // "1 hour 1 minute"

entries.name: dateDurationSpanEndDate
entries.description: Computes the end date for a duration span by adding the duration to the start time.

params.name: span
params.description: - Span whose end date should be computed.

entries.returns: Moment when the span ends.
entries.example:
const span = { startsAt: new Date('2024-01-01T10:00:00Z'), duration: 60 };
dateDurationSpanEndDate(span); // 2024-01-01T11:00:00Z

entries.name: durationSpanToDateRange
entries.description: Converts a {@link DateDurationSpan} to a {@link DateRange} with start and end dates.

params.name: span
params.description: - Span to project onto a start/end date range.

entries.returns: Range from startsAt to startsAt + duration.

entries.name: durationSpanFromDateRange
entries.description: Creates a {@link DateDurationSpan} from a {@link DateRange} by computing the duration in minutes between start and end.

params.name: dateRange
params.description: - Range to project onto a duration span.

entries.returns: Span anchored at the range's start with duration measured to its end.

entries.name: formatDateRangeFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable {@link FormatDateRangeFunction} from the given config or format function.

Useful when the same range formatting logic needs to be applied repeatedly with consistent settings.

params.name: inputConfig
params.description: - Format function or full configuration.

entries.returns: A reusable function that formats date ranges into strings.

entries.name: formatDateRange
entries.signature: formatDateRange(range: DateRange, inputConfig: FormatDateRangeFunctionConfigInput, separator?: string): string
entries.description: Convenience function that formats a date range in a single call without creating a reusable function.

params.name: range
params.description: - Date range to format.

params.name: inputConfig
params.description: - Format function or full configuration.

params.name: separator
params.description: - Optional separator override when inputConfig is a function.

entries.returns: The formatted date range string.

entries.name: formatDateRangeDistance
entries.description: Convenience function that computes the human-readable distance for a date range in a single call.

params.name: range
params.description: - Date range to compute distance for.

params.name: inputConfig
params.description: - Optional distance formatting configuration.

entries.returns: The human-readable distance string.

entries.name: formatToTimeRangeString
entries.signature: formatToTimeRangeString(startOrDateRange: DateRange | Date, end?: Maybe<Date>, onlyTimeRange?: boolean): string
entries.description:
Formats a date range as a time-only range string when both dates are on the same day,
or falls back to a full date+time range when they span multiple days.

Same day: `"12:00 AM - 4:00 PM"`
Different days: `"01/01/2001 12:00 AM - 01/02/2001 4:00 PM"`

params.name: startOrDateRange
params.description: - date range object or start date

params.name: end
params.description: - optional end date when a start date is provided

params.name: onlyTimeRange
params.description: - whether to force time-only formatting regardless of date span

entries.returns: the formatted time or date+time range string

entries.name: dateRangeDaysCount
entries.description:
Counts the total number of calendar days spanned by the range, inclusive of both endpoints.
Always returns at least 1, even for same-day ranges.

params.name: dateRange
params.description: - Range to measure.

entries.returns: Inclusive count of calendar days covered by the range.
entries.example:
const range = { start: new Date('2024-01-01'), end: new Date('2024-01-03') };
dateRangeDaysCount(range); // 3

entries.name: isDateRange
entries.description: Type guard to check if a value is a valid {@link DateRange} with both start and end as Date objects.

params.name: input
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: True if the value has valid Date `start` and `end` properties.
entries.example:
isDateRange({ start: new Date(), end: new Date() }); // true
isDateRange({ start: new Date() }); // false
isDateRange('not-a-range'); // false

entries.name: isSameDateRange
entries.description:
Compares two date ranges for exact millisecond equality on both start and end.
Returns true if both are nullish.

params.name: a
params.description: - First date range to compare.

params.name: b
params.description: - Second date range to compare.

entries.returns: True if both ranges are equal or both are nullish.

entries.name: isDate
entries.description: Type guard that checks whether the input is a Date instance.

params.name: input
params.description: - Value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the input is a Date.
entries.example:
isDate(new Date()); // true
isDate('2020-01-01'); // false

entries.name: msToMinutes
entries.description: Converts milliseconds to minutes.

params.name: milliseconds
params.description: - Duration in milliseconds.

entries.returns: Equivalent duration in minutes.
entries.example:
msToMinutes(60000); // 1
msToMinutes(120000); // 2

entries.name: msToSeconds
entries.description: Converts milliseconds to seconds.

params.name: milliseconds
params.description: - Duration in milliseconds.

entries.returns: Equivalent duration in seconds.
entries.example:
msToSeconds(1000); // 1
msToSeconds(2500); // 2.5

entries.name: hoursToMs
entries.description: Converts hours to milliseconds.

params.name: hours
params.description: - Number of hours (defaults to 1)

entries.returns: Equivalent duration in milliseconds.
entries.example:
hoursToMs(1); // 3600000
hoursToMs(2); // 7200000

entries.name: minutesToMs
entries.description: Converts minutes to milliseconds.

params.name: minutes
params.description: - Number of minutes (defaults to 1)

entries.returns: Equivalent duration in milliseconds.
entries.example:
minutesToMs(1); // 60000
minutesToMs(5); // 300000

entries.name: daysToMinutes
entries.description: Converts days to minutes.

params.name: days
params.description: - Number of days (defaults to 1)

entries.returns: Equivalent duration in minutes.
entries.example:
daysToMinutes(1); // 1440
daysToMinutes(7); // 10080

entries.name: isMaxFutureDate
entries.description: Checks whether the given date matches the {@link MAX_FUTURE_DATE} sentinel.

params.name: date
params.description: - Date to check.

entries.returns: Whether the date is the max future sentinel.
entries.example:
isMaxFutureDate(MAX_FUTURE_DATE); // true
isMaxFutureDate(new Date()); // false

entries.name: latestMinute
entries.description:
Returns the start of the current minute for the given time, effectively truncating seconds and milliseconds.

Defaults to the current time if no input is provided.

params.name: time
params.description: - Date to truncate (defaults to now)

entries.returns: Date rounded down to the start of the minute.

entries.name: toISODateString
entries.description: Converts the input to an ISO 8601 date string.

params.name: input
params.description: - Date or date string to convert.

entries.returns: The ISO 8601 string representation.

entries.name: guessCurrentTimezone
entries.description: Guesses the current system's timezone using the Intl API.
entries.returns: The IANA timezone string (e.g. "America/Chicago"), or undefined if detection fails.
entries.example:
const tz = guessCurrentTimezone();
// tz === 'America/New_York' (or similar)

entries.name: requireCurrentTimezone
entries.description:
Returns the current system's timezone, throwing if detection fails.

Convenience wrapper around {@link guessCurrentTimezone} for contexts where a timezone is required.
entries.returns: The IANA timezone string.
entries.example:
const tz = requireCurrentTimezone();
// tz === 'America/New_York' (guaranteed non-undefined)

entries.name: safeToJsDate
entries.description: Null-safe variant of {@link toJsDate} that returns undefined for null/undefined input.

params.name: input
params.description: - Date, date string, or null/undefined.

entries.returns: The parsed Date or undefined if input was nullish.
entries.example:
safeToJsDate('2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z'); // Date instance
safeToJsDate(undefined); // undefined

entries.name: toJsDate
entries.description: Converts the input to a JavaScript Date instance. Accepts Date objects, ISO 8601 strings, UTC date strings, and unix millisecond timestamps.

params.name: input
params.description: - Value to convert.

entries.returns: The parsed Date.
entries.example:
toJsDate('2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z'); // Date instance
toJsDate(new Date()); // same Date returned
toJsDate(1704067200000); // Date from unix ms

entries.name: parseJsDateString
entries.description:
Parses a date string or unix milliseconds timestamp into a Date, returning undefined if the result is invalid.

Prefers `parseISO` for ISO 8601 strings to avoid browser inconsistencies with `new Date()`.

params.name: input
params.description: - ISO 8601 string, UTC date string, or unix milliseconds.

entries.returns: The parsed Date, or undefined if invalid.
entries.example:
parseJsDateString('2020-04-30T00:00:00.000'); // Date instance
parseJsDateString('Sat, 03 Feb 2001 04:05:06 GMT'); // Date instance
parseJsDateString('not-a-date'); // undefined

entries.name: earliestDate
entries.description: Returns the earliest (minimum) date from the input array, ignoring null/undefined entries.

params.name: dates
params.description: - array of dates that may contain null/undefined

params.name: defaultDate
params.description: - fallback if no valid dates exist

entries.returns: the earliest date, or the default if the array has no valid dates

entries.name: latestDate
entries.description: Returns the latest (maximum) date from the input array, ignoring null/undefined entries.

params.name: dates
params.description: - array of dates that may contain null/undefined

params.name: defaultDate
params.description: - fallback if no valid dates exist

entries.returns: the latest date, or the default if the array has no valid dates

entries.name: isAfter
entries.signature: isAfter(a: Maybe<Date>, b: Maybe<Date>, defaultValue?: Maybe<boolean>): Maybe<boolean>
entries.description:
Null-safe date comparison that returns true when `a` is chronologically after `b`.

If either date is null/undefined, returns the default value instead.

params.name: a
params.description: - first date

params.name: b
params.description: - second date to compare against

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - returned when either date is nullish

entries.returns: whether `a` is after `b`, or the default

entries.name: isBefore
entries.signature: isBefore(a: Maybe<Date>, b: Maybe<Date>, defaultValue?: Maybe<boolean>): Maybe<boolean>
entries.description:
Null-safe date comparison that returns true when `a` is chronologically before `b`.

If either date is null/undefined, returns the default value instead.

params.name: a
params.description: - first date

params.name: b
params.description: - second date to compare against

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - returned when either date is nullish

entries.returns: whether `a` is before `b`, or the default

entries.name: isSameDate
entries.signature: isSameDate(a: Maybe<Date>, b: Maybe<Date>, defaultValue?: Maybe<boolean>): Maybe<boolean>
entries.description:
Null-safe exact date equality check (millisecond precision).

When both dates are null/undefined and no default is provided, returns true (treating two absent values as equal).

params.name: a
params.description: - first date

params.name: b
params.description: - second date

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - returned when either date is nullish (defaults to `a == b` if not provided)

entries.returns: whether the dates are exactly equal
entries.example:
const d = new Date('2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z');
isSameDate(d, new Date(d.getTime())); // true
isSameDate(null, null); // true
isSameDate(null, d); // false

entries.name: isSameDateDay
entries.signature: isSameDateDay(a: Maybe<Date>, b: Maybe<Date>, defaultValue?: Maybe<boolean>): Maybe<boolean>
entries.description:
Null-safe day-level date equality check (same year, month, and day, ignoring time).

When both dates are null/undefined and no default is provided, returns true (treating two absent values as equal).

params.name: a
params.description: - first date

params.name: b
params.description: - second date

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - returned when either date is nullish

entries.returns: whether both dates fall on the same calendar day

entries.name: roundDownToMinute
entries.description: Truncates seconds and milliseconds from the input date, effectively rounding down to the start of the minute.

params.name: date
params.description: - Moment to round; defaults to the current date/time.

entries.returns: Copy of the input with seconds and milliseconds set to zero.
entries.example:
const date = new Date('2024-01-01T12:30:45.123Z');
roundDownToMinute(date); // 2024-01-01T12:30:00.000Z

entries.name: roundDownToHour
entries.description: Truncates minutes, seconds, and milliseconds from the input date, effectively rounding down to the start of the hour.

params.name: date
params.description: - Moment to round; defaults to the current date/time.

entries.returns: Copy of the input with minutes, seconds, and milliseconds set to zero.
entries.example:
const date = new Date('2024-01-01T12:30:45.123Z');
roundDownToHour(date); // 2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z

entries.name: roundDateTo
entries.signature: roundDateTo(date: DateOrUnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber, roundToUnit: DateHourMinuteOrSecond, roundType?: FloorOrCeilRounding): DateOrUnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber
entries.description:
Rounds a date or unix timestamp to the nearest unit boundary using the specified rounding direction.

Preserves the input type: Date inputs return Date, number inputs return number.

params.name: date
params.description: - date or unix millisecond timestamp to round

params.name: roundToUnit
params.description: - time unit to round to ('hour', 'minute', or 'second')

params.name: roundType
params.description: - rounding direction ('floor' or 'ceil', defaults to 'floor')

entries.returns: the rounded value in the same type as the input
